# Abstract {-}

Multi-modal integration requires optimizing a shared representation that resolves dimensional and noise-level disparities across distinct sensor manifolds. While deep learning captures complex nonlinear structures, it often lacks direct feature attribution, complicating the validation of latent representations. We introduce Deep SiMR (Similarity-driven Multi-view Reconstruction), a framework (LEND, NED, NEDPP) that achieves deep multi-modal integration through auditable architectural constraints. We implement **Auditability by Design** by enforcing an Orthogonal Linear Embedding Entry (OLEE) — a direct linear projection $Z = XV$ — as the primary encoding operation. This constraint ensures that each latent component $Z$ is a direct linear combination of input features $X$, defined by an approximately orthonormal basis $V$. We also introduce a **Spectral Triage** mechanism that utilizes a **Spectral Sharpness** metric to identify and suppress high-dimensional noise components. This mechanism prevents isotropic accidental correlations from degrading the shared consensus, ensuring that the discovered representation represents structured signal. Synthetic benchmarks and clinical datasets demonstrate that this framework identifies linear subspaces that achieve performance parity with fully nonlinear architectures while maintaining the feature-level transparency necessary for scientific validation.
